title: dactylorhiza sambucina subsp. romana
intro: Dactylorhiza sambucina subsp. romana, also known as the Roman marsh orchid, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the orchid family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent qualities, making it useful for treating various health issues. The health benefits of Dactylorhiza sambucina subsp. romana include its ability to alleviate respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation. It can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in traditional medicine to make salves and creams.
